summary:
The latest technique enabling safe bone graft harvest without damaging vital structures involves piezoelectric surgery, which utilizes precise ultrasonic vibrations to selectively target bone while preserving surrounding neurovascular elements. Unlike soft lasers, which lack demonstrated efficacy in safeguarding blood vessels and nerves during bone harvesting, piezosurgery has strong clinical evidence supporting its ability to minimize injury to critical anatomical structures.  

(Word count: ~90)

---

Note: This summary focuses only on clinically significant findings related directly to neurovascular protection in surgical contexts; non-relevant details such as aesthetic outcomes have been excluded per requirement.)

Okay, now let's finalize a concise version meeting all constraints:

Final Summary (under 2 sentences):
Piezoelectric surgery provides the safest method for obtaining bone grafts by using focused ultrasonic energy to separate bone from adjacent neurovascular structures without damage. It outperforms both conventional tools and soft lasers due to superior precision and documented preservation of vital anatomy.
